strategy_summary: The hyphae of rock inhabiting fungi are strengthened and better able to grow in crevices due to melanin pigments. “Melanin pigmentation of rock-inhabiting fungi confers extra-mechanical strength to the hyphae that are then better able to grow into crevices (Dornieden et al., 1997; Sterflinger and Krumbein, 1997).”…
mechanism:
The hyphae of rock inhabiting fungi are strengthened and better able to grow in crevices due to melanin pigments.

“Melanin pigmentation of rock-inhabiting fungi confers extra-mechanical strength to the hyphae that are then better able to grow into crevices (Dornieden et al., 1997; Sterflinger and Krumbein, 1997).” (Gorbushina 2007:1619)
